H1.meaning: father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application
H1.kjv: chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.
H1.notes: Transliteration: ʼâb; Pronunciation: awb; Derivation: a primitive word;

H2.notes: Transliteration: ʼab; Pronunciation: ab; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1 (אָב)

H3.notes: Transliteration: ʼêb; Pronunciation: abe; Derivation: from the same as H24 (אָבִיב);

H4.notes: Transliteration: ʼêb; Pronunciation: abe; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3 (אֵב)

H5.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbagthâʼ; Pronunciation: ab-ag-thaw'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H6.meaning: properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)
H6.kjv: break, destroy(-uction), [phrase] not escape, fail, lose, (cause to, make) perish, spend, [idiom] and surely, take, be undone, [idiom] utterly, be void of, have no way to flee.
H6.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbad; Pronunciation: aw-bad'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H7.meaning: properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)
H7.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbad; Pronunciation: ab-ad'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H6 (אָבַד)

H8.notes: Transliteration: ʼôbêd; Pronunciation: o-bade'; Derivation: active of participle of H6 (אָבַד);

H9.meaning: concrete, something lost; abstract, destruction, i.e. Hades
H9.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbêdâh; Pronunciation: ab-ay-daw'; Derivation: from H6 (אָבַד); Compare H10 (אֲבַדֹּה).

H10.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbaddôh; Pronunciation: ab-ad-do'; Derivation: the same as H9 (אֲבֵדָה), miswritten for H11 (אֲבַדּוֹן);

H11.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbaddôwn; Pronunciation: ab-ad-done'; Derivation: intensive from H6 (אָבַד);

H12.notes: Transliteration: ʼabdân; Pronunciation: ab-dawn'; Derivation: from H6 (אָבַד);

H13.notes: Transliteration: ʼobdan; Pronunciation: ob-dawn'; Derivation: from H6 (אָבַד);

H14.meaning: to breathe after, i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent
H14.kjv: consent, rest content will, be willing.
H14.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbâh; Pronunciation: aw-baw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H15.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbeh; Pronunciation: aw-beh'; Derivation: from H14 (אָבָה);

H16.notes: Transliteration: ʼêbeh; Pronunciation: ay-beh'; Derivation: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of bending toward);

H17.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbôwy; Pronunciation: ab-o'ee; Derivation: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of desiring);

H18.notes: Transliteration: ʼêbûwç; Pronunciation: ay-booce'; Derivation: from H75 (אָבַס);

H19.notes: Transliteration: ʼibchâh; Pronunciation: ib-khaw'; Derivation: from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn);

H20.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbaṭṭîyach; Pronunciation: ab-at-tee'-akh; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H21.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîy; Pronunciation: ab-ee'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב); fatherly;

H22.meaning: Abiel, the name of two Israelites
H22.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyʼêl; Pronunciation: ab-ee-ale'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H410 (אֵל); father (i.e. possessor) of God;

H23.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyʼâçâph; Pronunciation: ab-ee-aw-sawf'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H622 (אָסַף); father of gathering (i.e. gatherer);

H24.meaning: green, i.e. a young ear of grain; hence, the name of the month Abib or Nisan
H24.kjv: Abib, ear, green ears of corn (not maize).
H24.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbîyb; Pronunciation: aw-beeb'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be tender);

H25.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîy Gibʻôwn; Pronunciation: ab-ee' ghib-one'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1391 (גִּבְעוֹן); father (i.e. founder) of Gibon;

H26.meaning: Abigail or Abigal, the name of two Israelitesses
H26.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîygayil; Pronunciation: ab-ee-gah'-yil; Derivation: or shorter אֲבִיגַל; from H1 (אָב) and H1524 (גִּיל); father (i.e. source) of joy;

H27.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîydân; Pronunciation: ab-ee-dawn'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1777 (דִּין); father of judgment (i.e. judge);

H28.meaning: Abida, a son of Abraham by Keturah
H28.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîydâʻ; Pronunciation: ab-ee-daw'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H3045 (יָדַע); father of knowledge (i.e. knowing);

H29.meaning: Abijah, the name of several Israelite men and two Israelitesses
H29.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyâh; Pronunciation: ab-ee-yaw'; Derivation: or prolonged אֲבִיָּהוּ ; from H1 (אָב) and H3050 (יָהּ); father (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;

H30.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyhûwʼ; Pronunciation: ab-ee-hoo'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1931 (הוּא); father (i.e. worshipper) of Him (i.e. God);

H31.meaning: Abihud, the name of two Israelites
H31.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyhûwd; Pronunciation: ab-ee-hood'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1935 (הוֹד); father (i.e. possessor) of renown;

H32.meaning: Abihail or Abichail, the name of three Israelites and two Israelitesses
H32.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyhayil; Pronunciation: ab-ee-hah'-yil; Derivation: or (more correctly) אֲבִיחַיִל ; from H1 (אָב) and H2428 (חַיִל); father (i.e. possessor) of might;

H33.meaning: an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer
H33.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîy hâ-ʻEzrîy; Pronunciation: ab-ee'-haw-ez-ree'; Derivation: from H44 (אֲבִיעֶזֶר) with the article inserted; father of the Ezrite;

H34.notes: Transliteration: ʼebyôwn; Pronunciation: eb-yone'; Derivation: from H14 (אָבָה), in the sense of want (especially in feeling);

H35.meaning: provocative of desire; the caper berry (from its stimulative taste)
H35.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbîyôwnâh; Pronunciation: ab-ee-yo-naw'; Derivation: from H14 (אָבָה);

H36.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyṭûwb; Pronunciation: ab-ee-toob'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H2898 (טוּב); father of goodness (i.e. good);

H37.meaning: Abital, a wife of King David
H37.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyṭâl; Pronunciation: ab-ee-tal'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H2919 (טַל); father of dew (i.e. fresh);

H38.meaning: Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah
H38.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyâm; Pronunciation: ab-ee-yawm'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H3220 (יָם); father of (the) sea (i.e. seaman);

H39.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîymâʼêl; Pronunciation: ab-ee-maw-ale'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and an elsewhere unused (probably foreign) word; father of Mael (apparently some Arab tribe);

H40.meaning: Abimelek, the name of two Philistine kings and of two Israelites
H40.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîymelek; Pronunciation: ab-ee-mel'-ek; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); father of (the) king;

H41.meaning: Abinadab, the name of four Israelites
H41.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîynâdâb; Pronunciation: ab-ee-naw-dawb'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5068 (נָדַב); father of generosity (i.e. liberal);

H42.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîynôʻam; Pronunciation: ab-ee-no'-am; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5278 (נֹעַם); father of pleasantness (i.e. gracious);

H43.notes: Transliteration: ʼEbyâçâph; Pronunciation: eb-yaw-sawf'; Derivation: contracted from H23 (אֲבִיאָסָף);

H44.meaning: Abiezer, the name of two Israelites
H44.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyʻezer; Pronunciation: ab-ee-ay'-zer; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5829 (עֵזֶר); father of help (i.e. helpful);

H45.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîy-ʻalbôwn; Pronunciation: ab-ee-al-bone'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and an unused root of uncertain derivation; probably, father of strength (i.e. valiant);

H46.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbîyr; Pronunciation: aw-beer'; Derivation: from H82 (אָבַר);

H47.kjv: angel, bull, chiefest, mighty (one), stout(-hearted), strong (one), valiant.
H47.notes: Transliteration: ʼabbîyr; Pronunciation: ab-beer'; Derivation: for H46 (אָבִיר)

H48.meaning: Abiram, the name of two Israelites
H48.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyrâm; Pronunciation: ab-ee-rawm'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7311 (רוּם); father of height (i.e. lofty);

H49.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyshag; Pronunciation: ab-ee-shag'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7686 (שָׁגָה); father of error (i.e. blundering);

H50.meaning: Abishua, the name of two Israelites
H50.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyshûwaʻ; Pronunciation: ab-ee-shoo'-ah; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7771 (שׁוֹעַ); father of plenty (i.e. prosperous);

H51.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyshûwr; Pronunciation: ab-ee-shoor'; Derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7791 (שׁוּר); father of (the) wall (i.e. perhaps mason);

H52.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyshay; Pronunciation: ab-ee-shah'ee; Derivation: or (shorter) אַבְשַׁי ; from H1 (אָב) and H7862 (שַׁי); father of a gift (i.e. probably generous);

H53.meaning: Abshalom, a son of David; also (the fuller form) a later Israelite
H53.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbîyshâlôwm; Pronunciation: ab-ee-shaw-lome'; Derivation: or (shortened) אַבְשָׁלוֹם ; from H1 (אָב) and H7965 (שָׁלוֹם); father of peace (i.e. friendly);

H54.notes: Transliteration: ʼEbyâthâr; Pronunciation: ab-yaw-thawr'; Derivation: contracted from H1 (אָב) and H3498 (יָתַר); father of abundance (i.e. liberal);

H55.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbak; Pronunciation: aw-bak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H56.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbal; Pronunciation: aw-bal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H57.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbêl; Pronunciation: aw-bale'; Derivation: from H56 (אָבַל);

H58.kjv: plain. Compare also the proper names beginning with Abel-.
H58.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbêl; Pronunciation: aw-bale'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be grassy);

H59.meaning: Abel, the name of two places in Palestine.
H59.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl; Pronunciation: aw-bale'; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל); a meadow;

H60.notes: Transliteration: ʼêbel; Pronunciation: ay'-bel; Derivation: from H56 (אָבַל);

H61.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbâl; Pronunciation: ab-awl'; Derivation: apparently from H56 (אָבַל) through the idea of negation;

H62.meaning: Abel of Beth-maakah, a place in Palestine
H62.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl Bêyth-Măʻakâh; Pronunciation: aw-bale' bayth ma-akaw'; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); meadow of Beth-Maakah;

H63.meaning: Abel hash-Shittim, a place in Palestine
H63.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl hash-Shiṭṭîym; Pronunciation: aw-bale' hash-shit-teem'; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H7848 (שִׁטָּה), with the article inserted; meadow of the acacias;

H64.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl Kᵉrâmîym; Pronunciation: aw-bale' ker-aw-meem'; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H3754 (כֶּרֶם); meadow of vineyards;

H65.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl Mᵉchôwlâh; Pronunciation: aw-bale' mekh-o-law'; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4246 (מְחֹלָה)xlit mᵉchôwlâh corrected to mᵉchôlâh; meadow of dancing;

H66.notes: Transliteration: ʼAbêl Mayim; Pronunciation: aw-bale' mah'-yim; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4325 (מַיִם); meadow of water;

H67.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂbêl Mitsrayim; Pronunciation: aw-bale' mits-rah'-yim; Derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4714 (מִצְרַיִם); meadow of Egypt;

H68.kjv: [phrase] carbuncle, [phrase] mason, [phrase] plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s).
H68.notes: Transliteration: ʼeben; Pronunciation: eh'-ben; Derivation: from the root of H1129 (בָּנָה) through the meaning to build;

H69.notes: Transliteration: ʼeben; Pronunciation: eh'-ben; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H68 (אֶבֶן)

H70.meaning: a pair of stones (only dual); a potter's wheel or a midwife's stool (consisting alike of two horizontal disks with a support between)
H70.notes: Transliteration: ʼôben; Pronunciation: o' ben; Derivation: from the same as H68 (אֶבֶן);

H71.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂbânâh; Pronunciation: ab-aw-naw'; Derivation: perhaps feminine of H68 (אֶבֶן); stony;  Compare H549 (אֲמָנָה).

H72.notes: Transliteration: ʼEben hâ-ʻÊzer; Pronunciation: eh'-ben haw-e'-zer; Derivation: from H68 (אֶבֶן) and H5828 (עֵזֶר) with the article inserted; stone of the help;

H73.notes: Transliteration: ʼabnêṭ; Pronunciation: ab-nate'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H74.notes: Transliteration: ʼAbnêr; Pronunciation: ab-nare'; Derivation: or (fully) אֲבִינֵר ; from H1 (אָב) and H5216 (נִיר); father of light (i.e. enlightening);

H75.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbaç; Pronunciation: aw-bas'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H76.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbaʻbuʻâh; Pronunciation: ab-ah-boo-aw'; Derivation: (by reduplication) from an unused root (meaning to belch forth);

H77.notes: Transliteration: ʼEbets; Pronunciation: eh'-bets; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to gleam; conspicuous;

H78.notes: Transliteration: ʼIbtsân; Pronunciation: ib-tsawn'; Derivation: from the same as H76 (אֲבַעְבֻּעָה); splendid;

H79.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbaq; Pronunciation: aw-bak'; Derivation: a primitive root, probably to float away (as vapor), but used only as denominative from H80 (אָבָק);

H80.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbâq; Pronunciation: aw-bawk'; Derivation: from root of H79 (אָבַק);

H81.notes: Transliteration: ʼăbâqâh; Pronunciation: ab-aw-kaw'; Derivation: feminine of H80 (אָבָק)

H82.notes: Transliteration: ʼâbar; Pronunciation: aw-bar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H83.notes: Transliteration: ʼêber; Pronunciation: ay-ber'; Derivation: from H82 (אָבַר);

H84.notes: Transliteration: ʼebrâh; Pronunciation: eb-raw'; Derivation: feminine of H83 (אֵבֶר)

H85.meaning: Abraham, the later name of Abram
H85.notes: Transliteration: ʼAbrâhâm; Pronunciation: ab-raw-hawm'; Derivation: contracted from H1 (אָב) and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude;

H86.notes: Transliteration: ʼabrêk; Pronunciation: ab-rake'; Derivation: probably an Egyptian word meaning

H87.meaning: Abram, the original name of Abraham
H87.notes: Transliteration: ʼAbrâm; Pronunciation: ab-rawm'; Derivation: contracted from H48 (אֲבִירָם); high father;

H88.meaning: Oboth, a place in the Desert
H88.notes: Transliteration: ʼôbôth; Pronunciation: o-both'; Derivation: plural of H178 (אוֹב); water-skins;

H89.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂgêʼ; Pronunciation: aw-gay'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation (compare H90 (אֲגַג));

H90.meaning: Agag, a title of Amalekitish kings
H90.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂgag; Pronunciation: ag-ag'; Derivation: or אֲגָג; of uncertain derivation (compare H89 (אָגֵא)); flame;

H91.meaning: an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag
H91.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂgâgîy; Pronunciation: ag-aw-ghee'; Derivation: patrial or patronymic from H90 (אֲגַג);

H92.meaning: a band, bundle, knot, or arch
H92.notes: Transliteration: ʼăguddâh; Pronunciation: ag-ood-daw'; Derivation: feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to bind);

H93.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕgôwz; Pronunciation: eg-oze'; Derivation: prob of Persian origin;

H94.meaning: Agur, a fanciful name for Solomon
H94.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂgûwr; Pronunciation: aw-goor'; Derivation: passive participle of H103 (אָגַר); gathered (i.e. received among the sages);

H95.meaning: properly, something gathered, i.e. perhaps a grain or berry; used only of a small (silver) coin
H95.notes: Transliteration: ʼăgôwrâh; Pronunciation: ag-o-raw'; Derivation: from the same as H94 (אָגוּר);

H96.notes: Transliteration: ʼegel; Pronunciation: eh'-ghel; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to flow down or together as drops);

H97.notes: Transliteration: ʼEglayim; Pronunciation: eg-lah'-yim; Derivation: dual of H96 (אֶגֶל).; a double pond;

H98.meaning: a marsh; hence a rush (as growing in swamps); hence a stockade of reeds
H98.notes: Transliteration: ʼăgam; Pronunciation: ag-am'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to collect as water);

H99.notes: Transliteration: ʼâgêm; Pronunciation: aw-game'; Derivation: probably from the same as H98 (אֲגַם) (in the sense of stagnant water);

H100.meaning: a bulrush (as growing there); collectively a rope of bulrushes
H100.notes: Transliteration: ʼagmôwn; Pronunciation: ag-mone'; Derivation: from the same as H98 (אֲגַם); a marshy pool (others from a different root, a kettle); by implication

H101.notes: Transliteration: ʼaggân; Pronunciation: ag-gawn'; Derivation: probably from H5059 (נָגַן);

H102.meaning: (only plural) wings of an army, or crowds of troops
H102.notes: Transliteration: ʼaggâph; Pronunciation: ag-gawf'; Derivation: probably from H5062 (נָגַף) (through the idea of impending); a cover or heap; i.e.

H103.notes: Transliteration: ʼâgar; Pronunciation: aw-gar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H104.meaning: an epistle (as carried by a state courier or postman)
H104.notes: Transliteration: ʼiggᵉrâʼ; Pronunciation: ig-er-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) of Persian origin;

H105.notes: Transliteration: ʼăgarṭâl; Pronunciation: ag-ar-tawl'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H106.notes: Transliteration: ʼegrôph; Pronunciation: eg-rofe'; Derivation: from H1640 (גָּרַף) (in the sense of grasping);

H107.notes: Transliteration: ʼiggereth; Pronunciation: ig-eh'-reth; Derivation: feminine of H104 (אִגְּרָא);

H108.notes: Transliteration: ʼêd; Pronunciation: ade; Derivation: from the same as H181 (אוּד) (in the sense of enveloping);

H109.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdab; Pronunciation: aw-dab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H110.notes: Transliteration: ʼAdbᵉʼêl; Pronunciation: ad-beh-ale'; Derivation: probably from H109 (אָדַב) (in the sense of chastisement) and H410 (אֵל); disciplined of God;

H111.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdad; Pronunciation: ad-ad'; Derivation: probably an orthographical variation for H2301 (חֲדַד);

H112.notes: Transliteration: ʼIddôw; Pronunciation: id-do; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H113.kjv: lord, master, owner. Compare also names beginning with 'Adoni-'.
H113.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdôwn; Pronunciation: aw-done'; Derivation: or (shortened) אָדֹן; from an unused root (meaning to rule);

H114.notes: Transliteration: ʼAddôwn; Pronunciation: ad-done'; Derivation: probably intensive for H113 (אָדוֹן); powerful;

H115.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdôwrayim; Pronunciation: ad-o-rah'-yim; Derivation: dual from H142 (אָדַר) (in the sense of eminence); double mound;

H116.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕdayin; Pronunciation: ed-ah'-yin; Derivation: (Aramaic) of uncertain derivation;

H117.kjv: excellent, famous, gallant, glorious, goodly, lordly, mighty(-ier one), noble, principal, worthy.
H117.notes: Transliteration: ʼaddîyr; Pronunciation: ad-deer'; Derivation: from H142 (אָדַר);

H118.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdalyâʼ; Pronunciation: ad-al-yaw'; Derivation: of Persian derivation;

H119.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdam; Pronunciation: aw-dam'; Derivation: to show blood (in the face), i.e.

H120.meaning: ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.)
H120.kjv: [idiom] another, [phrase] hypocrite, [phrase] common sort, [idiom] low, man (mean, of low degree), person.
H120.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdâm; Pronunciation: aw-dawm'; Derivation: from H119 (אָדַם);

H121.meaning: Adam the name of the first man, also of a place in Palestine
H121.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂdâm; Pronunciation: aw-dawm'; Derivation: the same as H120 (אָדָם);

H122.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdôm; Pronunciation: aw-dome'; Derivation: from H119 (אָדַם);

H123.meaning: Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region (Idumaea) occupied by him
H123.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔdôm; Pronunciation: ed-ome'; Derivation: or (fully) אֱדוֹם ; from H122 (אָדֹם); red (see Genesis 25:25);

H124.meaning: redness, i.e. the ruby, garnet, or some other red gem
H124.notes: Transliteration: ʼôdem; Pronunciation: o'-dem; Derivation: from H119 (אָדַם);

H125.notes: Transliteration: ʼădamdâm; Pronunciation: ad-am-dawm'; Derivation: reduplicated from H119 (אָדַם);

H126.meaning: Admah, a place near the Dead Sea
H126.notes: Transliteration: ʼAdmâh; Pronunciation: ad-maw'; Derivation: contracted for H127 (אֲדָמָה); earthy;

H127.notes: Transliteration: ʼădâmâh; Pronunciation: ad-aw-maw'; Derivation: from H119 (אָדַם);

H128.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdâmâh; Pronunciation: ad-aw-maw'; Derivation: the same as H127 (אֲדָמָה);

H129.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdâmîy; Pronunciation: ad-aw-mee'; Derivation: from H127 (אֲדָמָה); earthy;

H130.meaning: an Edomite, or descendants from (or inhabitants of) Edom
H130.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔdômîy; Pronunciation: ed-o-mee'; Derivation: or (fully) אֱדוֹמִי ; patronymic from H123 (אֱדֹם); See H726 (אֲרוֹמִי).

H131.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdummîym; Pronunciation: ad-oom-meem'; Derivation: plural of H121 (אָדָם); red spots;

H132.meaning: reddish (of the hair or the complexion)
H132.notes: Transliteration: ʼadmônîy; Pronunciation: ad-mo-nee'; Derivation: or (fully) אַדְמוֹנִי ; from H119 (אָדַם);

H133.notes: Transliteration: ʼAdmâthâʼ; Pronunciation: ad-maw-thaw'; Derivation: probably of Persian derivation;

H134.meaning: a basis (of a building, a column, etc.)
H134.notes: Transliteration: ʼeden; Pronunciation: eh'-den; Derivation: from the same as H113 (אָדוֹן) (in the sense of strength);

H135.notes: Transliteration: ʼAddân; Pronunciation: ad-dawn'; Derivation: intensive from the same as H134 (אֶדֶן); firm;

H136.meaning: the Lord (used as a proper name of God only)
H136.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônây; Pronunciation: ad-o-noy'; Derivation: an emphatic form of H113 (אָדוֹן);

H137.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônîy-Bezeq; Pronunciation: ad-o''-nee-beh'-zek; Derivation: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H966 (בֶּזֶק); lord of Bezek;

H138.meaning: Adonijah, the name of three Israelites
H138.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônîyâh; Pronunciation: ad-o-nee-yaw'; Derivation: original (prolonged) אֲדֹנִיָּהוּ ; from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H3050 (יָהּ); lord (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;

H139.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônîy-Tsedeq; Pronunciation: ad-o''-nee-tseh'-dek; Derivation: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H6664 (צֶדֶק); lord of justice;

H140.meaning: Adonikam, the name of one or two Israelites
H140.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônîyqâm; Pronunciation: ad-o-nee-kawm'; Derivation: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H6965 (קוּם); lord of rising (i.e. high);

H141.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdônîyrâm; Pronunciation: ad-o-nee-rawm'; Derivation: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H7311 (רוּם); lord of height;

H142.meaning: to expand, i.e. be great or (figuratively) magnificent
H142.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdar; Pronunciation: aw-dar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H143.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdâr; Pronunciation: ad-awr'; Derivation: probably of foreign derivation; perhaps meaning fire;

H144.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdâr; Pronunciation: ad-awr'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H143 (אֲדָר)

H145.meaning: amplitude, i.e. (concrete) a mantle; also (figuratively) splendor
H145.notes: Transliteration: ʼeder; Pronunciation: eh'-der; Derivation: from H142 (אָדַר);

H146.meaning: Addar, a place in Palestine; also an Israelite
H146.notes: Transliteration: ʼAddâr; Pronunciation: ad-dawr'; Derivation: intensive from H142 (אָדַר); ample;

H147.notes: Transliteration: ʼiddar; Pronunciation: id-dar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) intensive, from a root corresponding to H142 (אָדַר);

H148.notes: Transliteration: ʼădargâzêr; Pronunciation: ad-ar''-gaw-zare'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from the same as H147 (אִדַּר), and H1505 (גְּזַר);

H149.notes: Transliteration: ʼadrazdâʼ; Pronunciation: ad-raz-daw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) probably of Persian origin;

H150.notes: Transliteration: ʼădarkôn; Pronunciation: ad-ar-kone'; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H151.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdôrâm; Pronunciation: ad-o-rawm'; Derivation: contracted for H141 (אֲדֹנִירָם);

H152.meaning: Adrammelek, the name of an Assyrian idol, also of a son of Sennacherib
H152.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂdrammelek; Pronunciation: ad-ram-meh'-lek; Derivation: from H142 (אָדַר) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); splendor of (the) king;

H153.notes: Transliteration: ʼedrâʻ; Pronunciation: ed-raw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) an orthographical variation for H1872 (דְּרַע);

H154.meaning: Edrei, the name of two places in Palestine
H154.notes: Transliteration: ʼedreʻîy; Pronunciation: ed-reh'-ee; Derivation: from the equivalent of H153 (אֶדְרָע); mighty;

H155.meaning: something ample (as a large vine, a wide dress)
H155.notes: Transliteration: ʼaddereth; Pronunciation: ad-deh'-reth; Derivation: feminine of H117 (אַדִּיר); ; also the same as H145 (אֶדֶר)

H156.notes: Transliteration: ʼâdash; Pronunciation: aw-dash'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H157.meaning: to have affection for (sexually or otherwise)
H157.notes: Transliteration: ʼâhab; Pronunciation: aw-hab'; Derivation: or אָהֵב ; a primitive root;

H158.meaning: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H158.notes: Transliteration: ʼahab; Pronunciation: ah'-hab; Derivation: from H157 (אָהַב);

H159.meaning: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H159.notes: Transliteration: ʼôhab; Pronunciation: o'-hab; Derivation: from H156 (אָדַשׁ); meaning the same as H158 (אַהַב)

H160.meaning: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H160.notes: Transliteration: ʼahăbâh; Pronunciation: a-hab-aw; Derivation: feminine of H158 (אַהַב) and meaning the same

H161.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔhad; Pronunciation: o'-had; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to be united; unity;

H162.notes: Transliteration: ʼăhâhh; Pronunciation: a-haw'; Derivation: apparently a primitive word expressing pain exclamatorily;

H163.notes: Transliteration: ʼAhăvâʼ; Pronunciation: a-hav-aw'; Derivation: probably of foreign origin;

H164.meaning: Ehud, the name of two or three Israelites
H164.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊhûwd; Pronunciation: ay-hood'; Derivation: from the same as H161 (אֹהַד); united;

H165.kjv: I will be (Hosea 13:10,14) (which is often the rendering of the same Hebrew form from H1961 (הָיָה)).
H165.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕhîy; Pronunciation: e-hee'; Derivation: apparently an orthographical variation for H346 (אַיֵּה);

H166.notes: Transliteration: ʼâhal; Pronunciation: aw-hal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H167.notes: Transliteration: ʼâhal; Pronunciation: aw-hal'; Derivation: a denominative from H168 (אֹהֶל);

H168.meaning: a tent (as clearly conspicuous from a distance)
H168.notes: Transliteration: ʼôhel; Pronunciation: o'-hel; Derivation: from H166 (אָהַל);

H169.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔhel; Pronunciation: o'-hel; Derivation: the same as H168 (אֹהֶל);

H170.meaning: Oholah, a symbolic name for Samaria
H170.notes: Transliteration: ʼOhŏlâh; Pronunciation: o-hol-aw'; Derivation: in form a feminine of H168 (אֹהֶל), but in fact for אׇהֳלָהּ; from H168 (אֹהֶל); her tent (i.e. idolatrous sanctuary);

H171.notes: Transliteration: ʼOhŏlîyʼâb; Pronunciation: o''-hol-e-awb'; Derivation: from H168 (אֹהֶל) and H1 (אָב); tent of (his) father;

H172.meaning: Oholibah, a symbolic name for Judah
H172.notes: Transliteration: ʼOhŏlîybâh; Pronunciation: o''-hol-ee-baw'; Derivation: (similarly with H170 (אׇהֳלָה)) for אׇהֳלִיבָהּ ; from H168 (אֹהֶל); my tent (is) in her;

H173.notes: Transliteration: ʼOhŏlîybâmâh; Pronunciation: o''-hol-ee-baw-maw'; Derivation: from H168 (אֹהֶל) and H1116 (בָּמָה); tent of (the) height;

H174.notes: Transliteration: ʼăhâlîym; Pronunciation: a-haw-leem'; Derivation: or (feminine) אֲהָלוֹת (only used thus in the plural); of foreign origin;

H175.notes: Transliteration: ʼAhărôwn; Pronunciation: a-har-one'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H176.meaning: desire (and so probably in Proverbs 31:4); hence (by way of alternative) or, also if
H176.kjv: also, and, either, if, at the least, [idiom] nor, or, otherwise, then, whether.
H176.notes: Transliteration: ʼôw; Pronunciation: o; Derivation: presumed to be the 'constructive' or genitival form of אַו ; short for H185 (אַוָּה);

H177.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwʼêl; Pronunciation: oo-ale'; Derivation: from H176 (אוֹ) and H410 (אֵל); wish of God;

H178.meaning: properly, a mumble, i.e. a water skin (from its hollow sound); hence a necromancer (ventriloquist, as from a jar)
H178.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwb; Pronunciation: obe; Derivation: from the same as H1 (אָב) (apparently through the idea of prattling a father's name);

H179.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwbîyl; Pronunciation: o-beel'; Derivation: probably from H56 (אָבַל); mournful;

H180.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwbâl; Pronunciation: oo-bawl'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֻבָל ; from H2986 (יָבַל) (in the sense of H2988 (יָבָל));

H181.meaning: a poker (for turning or gathering embers)
H181.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwd; Pronunciation: ood; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to rake together;

H182.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwdôwth; Pronunciation: o-doth'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֹדוֹת (only thus in the plural); from the same as H181 (אוּד);

H183.kjv: covet, (greatly) desire, be desirous, long, lust (after).
H183.notes: Transliteration: ʼâvâh; Pronunciation: aw-vaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H184.notes: Transliteration: ʼâvâh; Pronunciation: aw-vaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H185.notes: Transliteration: ʼavvâh; Pronunciation: av-vaw'; Derivation: from H183 (אָוָה);

H186.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwzay; Pronunciation: oo-zah'-ee; Derivation: perhaps by permutation for H5813 (עֻזִּי), strong;

H187.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwzâl; Pronunciation: oo-zawl'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H188.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwy; Pronunciation: o'-ee; Derivation: probably from H183 (אָוָה) (in the sense of crying out after);

H189.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔvîy; Pronunciation: ev-ee'; Derivation: probably from H183 (אָוָה); desirous;

H190.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwyâh; Pronunciation: o-yaw'; Derivation: feminine of H188 (אוֹי)

H191.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕvîyl; Pronunciation: ev-eel'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be perverse);

H192.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔvîyl Mᵉrôdak; Pronunciation: ev-eel' mer-o-dak'; Derivation: of Aramaic derivation and probably meaning soldier of Merodak;

H193.meaning: the body (as being rolled together); also powerful
H193.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwl; Pronunciation: ool; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to twist, i.e. (by implication) be strong;

H194.kjv: if so be, may be, peradventure, unless.
H194.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwlay; Pronunciation: oo-lah'ee; Derivation: or (shortened) אֻלַי; from H176 (אוֹ);

H195.meaning: the Ulai (or Eulaeus), a river of Persia
H195.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwlay; Pronunciation: oo-lah'ee; Derivation: of Persian derivation;

H196.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕvilîy; Pronunciation: ev-ee-lee'; Derivation: from H191 (אֱוִיל);

H197.meaning: a vestibule (as bound to the building)
H197.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwlâm; Pronunciation: oo-lawm'; Derivation: or (shortened), אֻלָם ; from H481 (אָלַם) (in the sense of tying);

H198.meaning: Ulam, the name of two Israelites
H198.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwlâm; Pronunciation: oo-lawm'; Derivation: appar, from H481 (אָלַם) (in the sense of dumbness); solitary;

H199.kjv: as for, but, howbeit, in very deed, surely, truly, wherefore.
H199.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwlâm; Pronunciation: oo-lawm'; Derivation: apparently a variation of H194 (אוּלַי);

H200.notes: Transliteration: ʼivveleth; Pronunciation: iv-veh'-leth; Derivation: from the same as H191 (אֱוִיל);

H201.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwmâr; Pronunciation: o-mawr'; Derivation: from H559 (אָמַר); talkative;

H202.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwn; Pronunciation: one; Derivation: probably from the same as H205 (אָוֶן) (in the sense of effort, but successful);

H203.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwn; Pronunciation: one; Derivation: the same as H202 (אוֹן);

H204.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwn; Pronunciation: one; Derivation: or (shortened); אֹן; of Egyptian derivation;

H205.meaning: strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol
H205.kjv: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare H369 (אַיִן).
H205.notes: Transliteration: ʼâven; Pronunciation: aw-ven'; Derivation: from an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant (hence, to exert oneself, usually in vain; to come to naught);

H206.meaning: Aven, the contemptuous synonym of three places, one in Coele-Syria, one in Egypt (On), and one in Palestine (Bethel)
H206.kjv: Aven. See also H204 (אוֹן), H1007 (בֵּית אָוֶן).
H206.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂven; Pronunciation: aw'-ven; Derivation: the same as H205 (אָוֶן); idolatry;

H207.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwnôw; Pronunciation: o-no'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֹנוֹ; prolonged from H202 (אוֹן); strong;

H208.meaning: Onam, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite
H208.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwnâm; Pronunciation: o-nawm'; Derivation: a variation of H209 (אוֹנָן); strong;

H209.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwnân; Pronunciation: o-nawn'; Derivation: a variation of H207 (אוֹנוֹ); strong;

H210.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwphâz; Pronunciation: oo-fawz'; Derivation: perhaps a corruption of H211 (אוֹפִיר);

H211.meaning: Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East
H211.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔwphîyr; Pronunciation: o-feer'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֹפִיר; and אוֹפִר; of uncertain derivation;

H212.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwphân; Pronunciation: o-fawn'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֹפָן; from an unused root meaning to revolve;

H213.meaning: to press; (by implication) to be close, hurry, withdraw
H213.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwts; Pronunciation: oots; Derivation: a primitive root;

H214.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwtsâr; Pronunciation: o-tsaw'; Derivation: from H686 (אָצַר);

H215.meaning: to be (causative, make) luminous (literally and metaphorically)
H215.kjv: [idiom] break of day, glorious, kindle, (be, en-, give, show) light (-en, -ened), set on fire, shine.
H215.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwr; Pronunciation: ore; Derivation: a primitive root;

H216.meaning: illumination or (concrete) luminary (in every sense, including lightning, happiness, etc.)
H216.kjv: bright, clear, [phrase] day, light (-ning), morning, sun.
H216.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwr; Pronunciation: ore; Derivation: from H215 (אוֹר);

H217.meaning: flame; hence (in the plural) the East (as being the region of light)
H217.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwr; Pronunciation: ore; Derivation: from H215 (אוֹר);

H218.meaning: Ur, a place in Chaldaea; also an Israelite
H218.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwr; Pronunciation: oor; Derivation: the same as H217 (אוּר);

H219.meaning: luminousness, i.e. (figuratively) prosperity; also a plant (as being bright)
H219.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwrâh; Pronunciation: o-raw'; Derivation: feminine of H216 (אוֹר);

H220.notes: Transliteration: ʼăvêrâh; Pronunciation: av-ay-raw'; Derivation: by transposition for H723 (אֻרְוָה);

H221.meaning: Uri, the name of three Israelites
H221.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwrîy; Pronunciation: oo-ree'; Derivation: from H217 (אוּר); fiery;

H222.meaning: Uriel, the name of two Israelites
H222.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwrîyʼêl; Pronunciation: oo-ree-ale'; Derivation: from H217 (אוּר) and H410 (אֵל); flame of God;

H223.meaning: Urijah, the name of one Hittite and five Israelites
H223.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwrîyâh; Pronunciation: oo-ree-yaw'; Derivation: or (prolonged) אוּרִיָּהוּ ; from H217 (אוּר) and H3050 (יָהּ); flame of Jah;

H224.meaning: Urim, the oracular brilliancy of the figures in the high-priest's breastplate
H224.notes: Transliteration: ʼÛwrîym; Pronunciation: oo-reem'; Derivation: plural of H217 (אוּר); lights;

H225.meaning: properly, to come, i.e. (implied) to assent
H225.notes: Transliteration: ʼûwth; Pronunciation: ooth; Derivation: a primitive root;

H226.meaning: a signal (literally or figuratively), as aflag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
H226.notes: Transliteration: ʼôwth; Pronunciation: oth; Derivation: probably from H225 (אוּת) (in the sense of appearing);

H227.meaning: at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore
H227.kjv: beginning, for, from, hitherto, now, of old, once, since, then, at which time, yet.
H227.notes: Transliteration: ʼâz; Pronunciation: awz; Derivation: a demonstrative adverb;

H228.notes: Transliteration: ʼăzâʼ; Pronunciation: az-zaw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or אֲזָה (Aramaic);

H229.notes: Transliteration: ʼEzbay; Pronunciation: ez-bah'ee; Derivation: probably from H231 (אֵזוֹב); hyssop-like;

H230.notes: Transliteration: ʼăzâd; Pronunciation: az-zawd'; Derivation: (Aramaic) of uncertain derivation;

H231.notes: Transliteration: ʼêzôwb; Pronunciation: ay-zobe'; Derivation: probably of foreign derivation;

H232.meaning: something girt; a belt, also a band
H232.notes: Transliteration: ʼêzôwr; Pronunciation: ay-zore'; Derivation: from H246 (אֲזִקִּים);

H233.notes: Transliteration: ʼăzay; Pronunciation: az-ah'ee; Derivation: probably from H227 (אָז);

H234.notes: Transliteration: ʼazkârâh; Pronunciation: az-kaw-raw'; Derivation: from H2142 (זָכַר);

H235.meaning: to go away, hence, to disappear
H235.kjv: fail, gad about, go to and fro (but in Ezekiel 27:19 the word is rendered by many 'from Uzal,' by others 'yarn'), be gone (spent).
H235.notes: Transliteration: ʼâzal; Pronunciation: aw-zal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H236.notes: Transliteration: ʼăzal; Pronunciation: az-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) the same as H235 (אָזַל);

H237.meaning: Ezel, a memorial stone in Palestine
H237.notes: Transliteration: ʼezel; Pronunciation: eh'-zel; Derivation: from H235 (אָזַל); departure;

H238.meaning: to broaden out the ear (with the hand), i.e. (by implication) to listen
H238.kjv: give (perceive by the) ear, hear(-ken). See H239 (אָזַן).
H238.notes: Transliteration: ʼâzan; Pronunciation: aw-zan'; Derivation: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from H241 (אֹזֶן);

H239.notes: Transliteration: ʼâzan; Pronunciation: aw-zan'; Derivation: a primitive root (rather identical with H238 (אָזַן) through the idea of scales as if two ears);

H240.meaning: a spade or paddle (as having a broad end)
H240.notes: Transliteration: ʼâzên; Pronunciation: aw-zane'; Derivation: from H238 (אָזַן);

H241.meaning: broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man)
H241.kjv: [phrase] advertise, audience, [phrase] displease, ear, hearing, [phrase] show.
H241.notes: Transliteration: ʼôzen; Pronunciation: o'-zen; Derivation: from H238 (אָזַן);

H242.notes: Transliteration: ʼUzzên Sheʼĕrâh; Pronunciation: ooz-zane' sheh-er-aw'; Derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H7609 (שֶׁאֱרָה); plot of Sheerah (i.e. settled by him);

H243.notes: Transliteration: ʼAznôwth Tâbôwr; Pronunciation: az-noth' taw-bore'; Derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H8396 (תָּבוֹר); flats (i.e. tops) of Tabor (i.e. situated on it);

H244.meaning: Ozni, an Israelite; also an Oznite (collectively), his descendant
H244.notes: Transliteration: ʼOznîy; Pronunciation: oz-nee'; Derivation: from H241 (אֹזֶן); having (quick) ears;

H245.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂzanyâh; Pronunciation: az-an-yaw'; Derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H3050 (יָהּ); heard by Jah;

H246.notes: Transliteration: ʼăziqqîym; Pronunciation: az-ik-keem'; Derivation: a variation for H2131 (זִיקָה);

H247.notes: Transliteration: ʼâzar; Pronunciation: aw-zar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H248.notes: Transliteration: ʼezrôwaʻ; Pronunciation: ez-ro'-a; Derivation: a variation for H2220 (זְרוֹעַ);

H249.meaning: a spontaneous growth, i.e. native (tree or persons)
H249.kjv: bay tree, (home-) born (in the land), of the (one's own) country (nation).
H249.notes: Transliteration: ʼezrâch; Pronunciation: ez-rawkh'; Derivation: from H2224 (זָרַח) (in the sense of springing up);

H250.meaning: an Ezrachite or descendant of Zerach
H250.notes: Transliteration: ʼEzrâchîy; Pronunciation: ez-raw-khee'; Derivation: patronymic from H2246 (חֹבָב);

H251.meaning: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father))
H251.kjv: another, brother(-ly); kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'.
H251.notes: Transliteration: ʼâch; Pronunciation: awkh; Derivation: a primitive word;

H252.meaning: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance)
H252.notes: Transliteration: ʼach; Pronunciation: akh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H251 (אָח)

H253.notes: Transliteration: ʼâch; Pronunciation: awkh; Derivation: a variation for H162 (אֲהָהּ);

H254.notes: Transliteration: ʼâch; Pronunciation: awkh; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H255.meaning: a howler or lonesome wild animal
H255.notes: Transliteration: ʼôach; Pronunciation: o'-akh; Derivation: probably from H253 (אָח);

H256.meaning: Achab, the name of a king of Israel and of a prophet at Babylon
H256.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchʼâb; Pronunciation: akh-awb'; Derivation: once (by contraction) אֶחָב (Jeremiah 29:22); from H251 (אָח) and H1 (אָב); brother (i.e. friend) of (his) father;

H257.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchbân; Pronunciation: akh-bawn'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H995 (בִּין); brother (i.e. possessor) of understanding;

H258.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchad; Pronunciation: aw-khad'; Derivation: perhaps a primitive root;

H259.meaning: properly, united, i.e. one; or (as an ordinal) first
H259.kjv: a, alike, alone, altogether, and, any(-thing), apiece, a certain, (dai-) ly, each (one), [phrase] eleven, every, few, first, [phrase] highway, a man, once, one, only, other, some, together,
H259.notes: Transliteration: ʼechâd; Pronunciation: ekh-awd'; Derivation: a numeral from H258 (אָחַד);

H260.meaning: a bulrush or any marshy grass (particularly that along the Nile)
H260.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchûw; Pronunciation: aw'-khoo; Derivation: of uncertain (perhaps Egyptian) derivation;

H261.meaning: Echud, the name of three Israelites
H261.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊchûwd; Pronunciation: ay-khood'; Derivation: from H258 (אָחַד); united;

H262.notes: Transliteration: ʼachvâh; Pronunciation: akh-vaw'; Derivation: from H2331 (חָוָה) (in the sense of H2324 (חֲוָא));

H263.notes: Transliteration: ʼachăvâh; Pronunciation: akh-av-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H262 (אַחְוָה);

H264.notes: Transliteration: ʼachăvâh; Pronunciation: akh-av-aw'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח);

H265.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchôwach; Pronunciation: akh-o'-akh; Derivation: by reduplication from H251 (אָח); brotherly;

H266.meaning: an Achochite or descendant of Achoach
H266.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchôwchîy; Pronunciation: akh-o-khee'; Derivation: patronymic from H264 (אַחֲוָה);

H267.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchûwmay; Pronunciation: akh-oo-mah'-ee; Derivation: perhaps from H251 (אָח) and H4325 (מַיִם); brother (i.e. neighbour) of water;

H268.meaning: the hinder part; hence (adverb) behind, backward; also (as facing north) the West
H268.kjv: after(-ward), back (part, -side, -ward), hereafter, (be-) hind(-er part), time to come, without.
H268.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchôwr; Pronunciation: aw-khore'; Derivation: or (shortened) אָחֹר ; from H299 (אֲחִירַע);

H269.meaning: a sister (used very widely (like brother), literally and figuratively)
H269.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchôwth; Pronunciation: aw-khoth'; Derivation: irregular feminine of H251 (אָח);

H270.meaning: to seize (often with the accessory idea of holding in possession)
H270.kjv: [phrase] be affrighted, bar, (catch, lay, take) hold (back), come upon, fasten, handle, portion, (get, have or take) possess(-ion).
H270.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchaz; Pronunciation: aw-khaz'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H271.meaning: Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite
H271.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂchâz; Pronunciation: aw-khawz'; Derivation: from H270 (אָחַז); possessor;

H272.meaning: something seized, i.e. a possession (especially of land)
H272.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchuzzâh; Pronunciation: akh-ooz-zaw'; Derivation: feminine passive participle from H270 (אָחַז);

H273.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchzay; Pronunciation: akh-zah'ee; Derivation: from H270 (אָחַז); seizer;

H274.meaning: Achazjah, the name of a Jewish and an Israelite king
H274.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchazyâh; Pronunciation: akh-az-yaw'; Derivation: or (prolonged) אֲחַזְיָהוּ ; from H270 (אָחַז) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has seized;

H275.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchuzzâm; Pronunciation: akh-ooz-zawm'; Derivation: from H270 (אָחַז); seizure;

H276.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchuzzath; Pronunciation: akh-ooz-zath'; Derivation: a variation of H272 (אֲחֻזָּה); possession;

H277.meaning: Achi, the name of two Israelites
H277.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîy; Pronunciation: akh-ee'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח); brotherly;

H278.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊchîy; Pronunciation: ay-khee'; Derivation: probably the same as H277 (אֲחִי);

H279.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyʼâm; Pronunciation: akh-ee-awm'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H517 (אֵם); brother of the mother (i.e. uncle);

H280.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchîydâh; Pronunciation: akh-ee-daw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H2420 (חִידָה),

H281.meaning: Achijah, the name of nine Israelites
H281.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyâh; Pronunciation: akh-ee-yaw; Derivation: or (prolonged) אֲחִיָּהוּ ; from H251 (אָח) and H3050 (יָהּ); brother (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;

H282.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyhûwd; Pronunciation: akh-ee-hood'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H1935 (הוֹד); brother (i.e. possessor) of renown;

H283.meaning: Achio, the name of three Israelites
H283.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchyôw; Pronunciation: akh-yo'; Derivation: prolonged from H251 (אָח); brotherly;

H284.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîychud; Pronunciation: akh-ee-khood'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H2330 (חוּד); brother of a riddle (i.e. mysterious);

H285.meaning: Achitub, the name of several priests
H285.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyṭûwb; Pronunciation: akh-ee-toob'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H2898 (טוּב); brother of goodness;

H286.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîylûwd; Pronunciation: akh-ee-lood'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H3205 (יָלַד); brother of one born;

H287.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîymôwth; Pronunciation: akh-ee-moth'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H4191 (מוּת); brother of death;

H288.meaning: Achimelek, the name of an Israelite and of a Hittite
H288.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîymelek; Pronunciation: akh-ee-meh'-lek; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); brother of (the) king;

H289.meaning: Achiman, the name of an Anakite and of an Israelite
H289.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyman; Pronunciation: akh-ee-man'; Derivation: or אֲחִימָן ; from H251 (אָח) and H4480 (מִן); brother of a portion (i.e. gift);

H290.meaning: Achimaats, the name of three Israelites
H290.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîymaʻats; Pronunciation: akh-ee-mah'-ats; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and the equivalent of H4619 (מַעַץ); brother of anger;

H291.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchyân; Pronunciation: akh-yawn'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח); brotherly;

H292.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîynâdâb; Pronunciation: akh-ee-naw-dawb'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5068 (נָדַב); brother of liberality;

H293.meaning: Achinoam, the name of two Israelitesses
H293.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîynôʻam; Pronunciation: akh-ee-no'-am; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5278 (נֹעַם); brother of pleasantness;

H294.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyçâmâk; Pronunciation: akh-ee-saw-mawk'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5564 (סָמַךְ); brother of support;

H295.meaning: Achiezer, the name of two Israelites
H295.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyʻezer; Pronunciation: akh-ee-eh'-zer; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); brother of help;

H296.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyqâm; Pronunciation: akh-ee-kawm'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H6965 (קוּם); brother of rising (i.e. high);

H297.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyrâm; Pronunciation: akh-ee-rawm'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7311 (רוּם); brother of height (i.e. high);

H298.meaning: an Achiramite or descendant (collectively) of Achiram
H298.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyrâmîy; Pronunciation: akh-ee-raw-mee'; Derivation: patronymic from H297 (אֲחִירָם);

H299.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyraʻ; Pronunciation: akh-ee-rah'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7451 (רַע); brother of wrong;

H300.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyshachar; Pronunciation: akh-ee-shakh'-ar; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7837 (שַׁחַר); brother of (the) dawn;

H301.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîyshâr; Pronunciation: akh-ee-shawr'; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7891 (שִׁיר); brother of (the) singer;

H302.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchîythôphel; Pronunciation: akh-ee-tho'-fel; Derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H8602 (תָּפֵל); brother of folly;

H303.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchlâb; Pronunciation: akh-lawb'; Derivation: from the same root as H2459 (חֶלֶב); fatness (i.e. fertile);

H304.meaning: Achlai, the name of an Israelitess and of an Israelite
H304.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchlay; Pronunciation: akh-lah'ee; Derivation: the same as H305 (אַחֲלַי); wishful;

H305.notes: Transliteration: ʼachălay; Pronunciation: akh-al-ah'ee; Derivation: or אַחֲלֵי; prob from H253 (אָח) and a variation of H3863 (לוּא);

H306.notes: Transliteration: ʼachlâmâh; Pronunciation: akh-law'-maw; Derivation: perhaps from H2492 (חָלַם) (and thus dream-stone);

H307.meaning: Achmetha (i.e. Ecbatana), the summer capital of Persia
H307.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchmᵉthâʼ; Pronunciation: akh-me-thaw'; Derivation: of Persian derivation;

H308.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchaçbay; Pronunciation: akh-as-bah'ee; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H309.meaning: to loiter (i.e. be behind); by implication to procrastinate
H309.kjv: continue, defer, delay, hinder, be late (slack), stay (there), tarry (longer).
H309.notes: Transliteration: ʼâchar; Pronunciation: aw-khar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H310.meaning: properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after (in various senses)
H310.kjv: after (that, -ward), again, at, away from, back (from, -side), behind, beside, by, follow (after, -ing), forasmuch, from, hereafter, hinder end, [phrase] out (over) live, [phrase] persecute, posterity, pursuing, remnant, seeing, since, thence(-forth), when, with.
H310.notes: Transliteration: ʼachar; Pronunciation: akh-ar'; Derivation: from H309 (אָחַר);

H311.notes: Transliteration: ʼachar; Pronunciation: akh-ar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H310 (אַחַר);

H312.meaning: properly, hinder; generally, next, other, etc.
H312.notes: Transliteration: ʼachêr; Pronunciation: akh-air'; Derivation: from H309 (אָחַר);

H313.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchêr; Pronunciation: akh-air'; Derivation: the same as H312 (אַחֵר);

H314.meaning: hinder; generally, late or last; specifically (as facing the east) western
H314.kjv: after (-ward), to come, following, hind(-er, -ermost, -most), last, latter, rereward, ut(ter) most.
H314.notes: Transliteration: ʼachărôwn; Pronunciation: akh-ar-one'; Derivation: or (shortened) אַחֲרֹן; from H309 (אָחַר);

H315.notes: Transliteration: ʼAchrach; Pronunciation: akh-rakh'; Derivation: from H310 (אַחַר) and H251 (אָח); after (his) brother;

H316.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂcharchêl; Pronunciation: akh-ar-kale'; Derivation: from H310 (אַחַר) and H2426 (חֵיל); behind (the) intrenchment (i.e. safe);

H317.notes: Transliteration: ʼochŏrîy; Pronunciation: okh-or-ee'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H311 (אַחַר);

H318.notes: Transliteration: ʼochŏrêyn; Pronunciation: okh-or-ane'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or (shortened) אׇחֳרֵן; (Aramaic) from H317 (אׇחֳרִי);

H319.meaning: the last or end, hence, the future; also posterity
H319.kjv: (last, latter) end (time), hinder (utter) -most, length, posterity, remnant, residue, reward.
H319.notes: Transliteration: ʼachărîyth; Pronunciation: akh-ar-eeth'; Derivation: from H310 (אַחַר);

H320.notes: Transliteration: ʼachărîyth; Pronunciation: akh-ar-eeth'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H311 (אַחַר); the same as H319 (אַחֲרִית);

H321.notes: Transliteration: ʼochŏrân; Pronunciation: okh-or-awn'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H311 (אַחַר); the same as H317 (אׇחֳרִי);

H322.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchôrannîyth; Pronunciation: akh-o-ran-neeth'; Derivation: prolonged from H268 (אָחוֹר);

H323.meaning: a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia)
H323.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchashdarpan; Pronunciation: akh-ash-dar-pan'; Derivation: of Persian derivation;

H324.meaning: a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia)
H324.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchashdarpan; Pronunciation: akh-ash-dar-pan'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H323 (אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפַּן)

H325.meaning: Achashverosh (i.e. Ahasuerus or Artaxerxes, but in this case Xerxes), the title (rather than name) of a Persian king
H325.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂchashvêrôwsh; Pronunciation: akh-ash-vay-rosh'; Derivation: or (shortened) אַחַשְׁרֹשׁ (Esther 10:1); of Persian origin;

H326.meaning: an achastarite (i.e. courier); the designation (rather than name) of an Israelite
H326.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchashtârîy; Pronunciation: akh-ash-taw-ree'; Derivation: probably of Persian derivation;

H327.notes: Transliteration: ʼăchashtârân; Pronunciation: akh-ash-taw-rawn'; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H328.meaning: (as a noun) a necromancer (from their soft incantations), (as an adverb) gently
H328.notes: Transliteration: ʼaṭ; Pronunciation: at; Derivation: from an unused root perhaps meaning to move softly;

H329.notes: Transliteration: ʼâṭâd; Pronunciation: aw-tawd'; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to pierce or make fast;

H330.notes: Transliteration: ʼêṭûwn; Pronunciation: ay-toon'; Derivation: from an unused root (probably meaning to bind);

H331.meaning: to close (the lips or ears); by analology to contract (a window by bevelled jambs)
H331.notes: Transliteration: ʼâṭam; Pronunciation: aw-tam'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H332.notes: Transliteration: ʼâṭar; Pronunciation: aw-tar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H333.meaning: Ater, the name of three Israelites
H333.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂṭêr; Pronunciation: aw-tare'; Derivation: from H332 (אָטַר); maimed;

H334.meaning: shut up, i.e. impeded (as to the use of the right hand)
H334.notes: Transliteration: ʼiṭṭêr; Pronunciation: it-tare'; Derivation: from H332 (אָטַר);

H335.kjv: how, what, whence, where, whether, which (way).
H335.notes: Transliteration: ʼay; Pronunciation: ah'ee; Derivation: perhaps from H370 (אַיִן);

H336.notes: Transliteration: ʼîy; Pronunciation: ee; Derivation: probably identical with H335 (אַי) (through the idea of a query);

H337.notes: Transliteration: ʼîy; Pronunciation: ee; Derivation: short from H188 (אוֹי);

H338.meaning: a howler (used only in the plural), i.e. any solitary wild creature
H338.notes: Transliteration: ʼîy; Pronunciation: ee; Derivation: probably identical with H337 (אִי) (through the idea of a doleful sound);

H339.meaning: properly, a habitable spot (as desirable); dry land, a coast, an island
H339.notes: Transliteration: ʼîy; Pronunciation: ee; Derivation: from H183 (אָוָה);

H340.meaning: to hate (as one of an opposite tribe or party); hence to be hostile
H340.notes: Transliteration: ʼâyab; Pronunciation: aw-yab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H341.notes: Transliteration: ʼôyêb; Pronunciation: o-yabe'; Derivation: or (fully) אוֹיֵב; active participle of H340 (אָיַב);

H342.notes: Transliteration: ʼêybâh; Pronunciation: ay-baw'; Derivation: from H340 (אָיַב);

H343.notes: Transliteration: ʼêyd; Pronunciation: ade; Derivation: from the same as H181 (אוּד) (in the sense of bending down);

H344.notes: Transliteration: ʼayâh; Pronunciation: ah-yaw'; Derivation: perhaps from H337 (אִי);

H345.meaning: Ajah, the name of two Israelites
H345.notes: Transliteration: ʼAyâh; Pronunciation: ah-yaw'; Derivation: the same as H344 (אַיָּה);

H346.notes: Transliteration: ʼayêh; Pronunciation: ah-yay'; Derivation: prolonged from H335 (אַי);

H347.meaning: Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience
H347.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎyôwb; Pronunciation: ee-yobe'; Derivation: from H340 (אָיַב); hated (i.e. persecuted);

H348.meaning: Izebel, the wife of king Ahab
H348.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎyzebel; Pronunciation: ee-zeh'-bel; Derivation: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H2083 (זְבֻל);

H349.notes: Transliteration: ʼêyk; Pronunciation: ake; Derivation: also אֵיכָה ; and אֵיכָכָה ; prolonged from H335 (אַי);

H350.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎy-kâbôwd; Pronunciation: ee-kaw-bode'; Derivation: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H3519 (כָּבוֹד); (there is) no glory, i.e. inglorious;

H351.notes: Transliteration: ʼêykôh; Pronunciation: ay-ko; Derivation: probably a variation for H349 (אֵיךְ), but not as an interogative;

H352.meaning: properly, strength; hence, anything strong; specifically a chief (politically); also a ram (from his strength); a pilaster (as a strong support); an oak or other strong tree
H352.kjv: mighty (man), lintel, oak, post, ram, tree.
H352.notes: Transliteration: ʼayil; Pronunciation: ah'-yil; Derivation: from the same as H193 (אוּל);

H353.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕyâl; Pronunciation: eh-yawl'; Derivation: a variation of H352 (אַיִל);

H354.notes: Transliteration: ʼayâl; Pronunciation: ah-yawl'; Derivation: an intensive form of H352 (אַיִל) (in the sense of ram);

H355.notes: Transliteration: ʼayâlâh; Pronunciation: ah-yaw-law'; Derivation: feminine of H354 (אַיָּל);

H356.meaning: Elon, the name of a place in Palestine, and also of one Hittite, two Israelites
H356.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊylôwn; Pronunciation: ay-lone'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֵלוֹן ; or אֵילֹן ; from H352 (אַיִל); oak-grove;

H357.meaning: Ajalon, the name of five places in Palestine
H357.notes: Transliteration: ʼAyâlôwn; Pronunciation: ah-yaw-lone'; Derivation: from H354 (אַיָּל); deer-field;

H358.meaning: Elon of Beth-chanan, a place in Palestine
H358.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊylôwn Bêyth Chânân; Pronunciation: ay-lone' bayth-chawnawn'; Derivation: from H356 (אֵילוֹן), H1004 (בַּיִת), and H2603 (חָנַן); oak-grove of (the) house of favor;

H359.meaning: Eloth or Elath, a place on the Red Sea
H359.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊylôwth; Pronunciation: ay-loth'; Derivation: or אֵילַת ; from H352 (אַיִל); trees or a grove (i.e. palms);

H360.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕyâlûwth; Pronunciation: eh-yaw-looth'; Derivation: feminine of H353 (אֱיָל);

H361.meaning: a pillar-space (or colonnade), i.e. a pale (or portico)
H361.notes: Transliteration: ʼêylâm; Pronunciation: ay-lawm'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֵלָם ; or (feminine) אֵלַמָּה ; probably from H352 (אַיִל);

H362.meaning: Elim, a place in the Desert
H362.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊylim; Pronunciation: ay-leem'; Derivation: plural of H352 (אַיִל); palm-trees;

H363.notes: Transliteration: ʼîylân; Pronunciation: ee-lawn'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H356 (אֵילוֹן);

H364.meaning: El-Paran, a portion of the district of Paran
H364.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊyl Pâʼrân; Pronunciation: ale paw-rawn'; Derivation: from H352 (אַיִל) and H6290 (פָּארָן); oak of Paran;

H365.notes: Transliteration: ʼayeleth; Pronunciation: ah-yeh'-leth; Derivation: the same as H355 (אַיָּלָה);

H366.notes: Transliteration: ʼâyôm; Pronunciation: aw-yome'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to frighten);

H367.meaning: fright; concrete, an idol (as a bugbear)
H367.kjv: dread, fear, horror, idol, terrible, terror.
H367.notes: Transliteration: ʼêymâh; Pronunciation: ay-maw'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֵמָה; from the same as H366 (אָיֹם);

H368.meaning: Emim, an early Canaanitish (or Maobitish) tribe
H368.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊymîym; Pronunciation: ay-meem'; Derivation: plural of H367 (אֵימָה); terrors;

H369.meaning: a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle
H369.kjv: else, except, fail, (father-) less, be gone, in(-curable), neither, never, no (where), none, nor, (any, thing), not, nothing, to nought, past, un(-searchable), well-nigh, without. Compare H370 (אַיִן).
H369.notes: Transliteration: ʼayin; Pronunciation: ah'-yin; Derivation: as if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist;

H370.meaning: where? (only in connection with prepositional prefix, whence)
H370.notes: Transliteration: ʼayin; Pronunciation: ah-yin'; Derivation: probably identical with H369 (אַיִן) in the sense of query (compare H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי)

H371.notes: Transliteration: ʼîyn; Pronunciation: een; Derivation: apparently a shortened form of H369 (אַיִן); but (like H370 (אַיִן)) an interrogative;

H372.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎyʻezêr; Pronunciation: ee-eh'-zer; Derivation: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H5828 (עֵזֶר); helpless;

H373.meaning: an Iezrite or descendant of Iezer
H373.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎyʻezrîy; Pronunciation: ee-ez-ree'; Derivation: patronymic from H372 (אִיעֶזֵר);

H374.meaning: an ephah or measure for grain; hence, a measure in general
H374.notes: Transliteration: ʼêyphâh; Pronunciation: ay-faw'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֵפָה ; of Egyptian derivation;

H375.meaning: what place?; also (of time) when?; or (of means) how?
H375.notes: Transliteration: ʼêyphôh; Pronunciation: ay-fo'; Derivation: from H335 (אַי) and H6311 (פֹּה);

H376.meaning: a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term (and in such cases frequently not expressed in translation)
H376.kjv: also, another, any (man), a certain, [phrase] champion, consent, each, every (one), fellow, (foot-, husband-) man, (good-, great, mighty) man, he, high (degree), him (that is), husband, man(-kind), [phrase] none, one, people, person, [phrase] steward, what (man) soever, whoso(-ever), worthy. Compare H802 (אִשָּׁה).
H376.notes: Transliteration: ʼîysh; Pronunciation: eesh; Derivation: contracted for H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ) (or perhaps rather from an unused root meaning to be extant);

H377.meaning: to be a man, i.e. act in amanly way
H377.notes: Transliteration: ʼîysh; Pronunciation: eesh; Derivation: denominative from H376 (אִישׁ);

H378.meaning: Ish-Bosheth, a son of King Saul
H378.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎysh-Bôsheth; Pronunciation: eesh-bo'-sheth; Derivation: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1322 (בֹּשֶׁת); man of shame;

H379.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎyshᵉhôwd; Pronunciation: eesh-hode'; Derivation: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1935 (הוֹד); man of renown;

H380.meaning: the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle (of night)
H380.notes: Transliteration: ʼîyshôwn; Pronunciation: ee-shone'; Derivation: diminutive from H376 (אִישׁ);

H382.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎysh-Ṭôwb; Pronunciation: eesh-tobe'; Derivation: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H2897 (טוֹב); man of Tob;

H383.meaning: properly, entity; used only as aparticle of affirmation, there is
H383.kjv: art thou, can, do ye, have, it be, there is (are), [idiom] we will not.
H383.notes: Transliteration: ʼîythay; Pronunciation: ee-thah'ee; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3426 (יֵשׁ);

H384.meaning: Ithiel, the name of an Israelite, also of a symbolical person
H384.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎythîyʼêl; Pronunciation: eeth-ee-ale'; Derivation: perhaps from H837 (אֹשֶׁר) and H410 (אֵל); God has arrived;

H385.notes: Transliteration: ʼÎythâmâr; Pronunciation: eeth-aw-mawr'; Derivation: from H339 (אִי) and H8558 (תָּמָר); coast of the palm-tree;

H386.meaning: permanence; hence (concrete) permanent; specifically a chieftain
H386.notes: Transliteration: ʼêythân; Pronunciation: ay-thawn'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֵתָן ; from an unused root (meaning to continue);

H387.meaning: Ethan, the name of four Israelites
H387.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊythân; Pronunciation: ay-thawn'; Derivation: the same as H386 (אֵיתָן); permanent;

H388.meaning: Ethanim, the name of a month
H388.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊythânîym; Pronunciation: ay-thaw-neem'; Derivation: plural of H386 (אֵיתָן); always with the article; the permanent brooks;

H389.meaning: a particle of affirmation, surely; hence (by limitation) only
H389.kjv: also, in any wise, at least, but, certainly, even, howbeit, nevertheless, notwithstanding, only, save, surely, of a surety, truly, verily, [phrase] wherefore, yet (but).
H389.notes: Transliteration: ʼak; Pronunciation: ak; Derivation: akin to H403 (אָכֵן);

H390.notes: Transliteration: ʼAkkad; Pronunciation: ak-kad'; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to strengthen; a fortress;

H391.notes: Transliteration: ʼakzâb; Pronunciation: ak-zawb'; Derivation: from H3576 (כָּזַב);

H392.meaning: Akzib, the name of two places in Palestine
H392.notes: Transliteration: ʼAkzîyb; Pronunciation: ak-zeeb'; Derivation: from H391 (אַכְזָב); deceitful (in the sense of a winter-torrent which fails in summer);

H393.meaning: violent; by implication deadly; also (in a good sense) brave
H393.notes: Transliteration: ʼakzâr; Pronunciation: ak-zawr'; Derivation: from an unused root (apparently meaning to act harshly);

H394.notes: Transliteration: ʼakzârîy; Pronunciation: ak-zawr-ree'; Derivation: from H393 (אַכְזָר);

H395.notes: Transliteration: ʼakzᵉrîyûwth; Pronunciation: ak-ze-ree-ooth'; Derivation: from H394 (אַכְזָרִי);

H396.notes: Transliteration: ʼăkîylâh; Pronunciation: ak-ee-law'; Derivation: feminine from H398 (אָכַל);

H397.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂkîysh; Pronunciation: aw-keesh'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H398.kjv: [idiom] at all, burn up, consume, devour(-er, up), dine, eat(-er, up), feed (with), food, [idiom] freely, [idiom] in...wise(-deed, plenty), (lay) meat, [idiom] quite.
H398.notes: Transliteration: ʼâkal; Pronunciation: aw-kal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H399.notes: Transliteration: ʼăkal; Pronunciation: ak-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H398 (אָכַל)

H400.kjv: eating, food, meal(-time), meat, prey, victuals.
H400.notes: Transliteration: ʼôkel; Pronunciation: o'-kel; Derivation: from H398 (אָכַל);

H401.notes: Transliteration: ʼUkâl; Pronunciation: oo-kawl'; Derivation: or אֻכָּל; apparently from H398 (אָכַל); devoured;

H402.notes: Transliteration: ʼoklâh; Pronunciation: ok-law'; Derivation: feminine of H401 (אֻכָל);

H403.kjv: but, certainly, nevertheless, surely, truly, verily.
H403.notes: Transliteration: ʼâkên; Pronunciation: aw-kane'; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן) (compare H3651 (כֵּן));

H404.notes: Transliteration: ʼâkaph; Pronunciation: aw-kaf'; Derivation: a primitive root; apparently meaning to curve (as with a burden);

H405.meaning: a load; by implication, a stroke (others dignity)
H405.notes: Transliteration: ʼekeph; Pronunciation: eh'-kef; Derivation: from H404 (אָכַף);

H406.notes: Transliteration: ʼikkâr; Pronunciation: ik-kawr'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to dig;

H407.notes: Transliteration: ʼAkshâph; Pronunciation: ak-shawf'; Derivation: from H3784 (כָּשַׁף); fascination;

H408.meaning: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing
H408.kjv: nay, neither, [phrase] never, no, nor, not, nothing (worth), rather than.
H408.notes: Transliteration: ʼal; Pronunciation: al; Derivation: a negative particle (akin to H3808 (לֹא));

H409.meaning: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing
H409.notes: Transliteration: ʼal; Pronunciation: al; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H408 (אַל)

H410.meaning: strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
H410.kjv: God (god), [idiom] goodly, [idiom] great, idol, might(-y one), power, strong. Compare names in '-el.'
H410.notes: Transliteration: ʼêl; Pronunciation: ale; Derivation: shortened from H352 (אַיִל);

H411.notes: Transliteration: ʼêl; Pronunciation: ale; Derivation: a demonstrative particle (but only in a plural sense)

H412.notes: Transliteration: ʼêl; Pronunciation: ale; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H411 (אֵל)

H413.meaning: near, with or among; often in general, to
H413.kjv: about, according to, after, against, among, as for, at, because(-fore, -side), both...and, by, concerning, for, from, [idiom] hath, in(-to), near, (out) of, over, through, to(-ward), under, unto, upon, whether, with(-in).

H414.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊlâʼ; Pronunciation: ay-law'; Derivation: a variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; oak;

H415.meaning: El-Elohi-Jisrael, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob
H415.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊl ʼĕlôhêy Yisrâʼêl; Pronunciation: ale el-o-hay' yis-rawale'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H430 (אֱלֹהִים) and H3478 (יִשְׂרָאֵל); the mighty god if Jisrael;

H416.meaning: El-Bethel, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob
H416.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊl Bêyth-ʼÊl; Pronunciation: ale bayth-ale'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1008 (בֵּית־אֵל); the God of Bethel;

H417.notes: Transliteration: ʼelgâbîysh; Pronunciation: el-gaw-beesh'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1378 (גָּבִישׁ);

H418.notes: Transliteration: ʼalgûwmmîym; Pronunciation: al-goom-meem'; Derivation: by transposition for H484 (אַלְמֻגִּים);

H419.notes: Transliteration: ʼEldâd; Pronunciation: el-dad'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1730 (דּוֹד); God has loved;

H420.notes: Transliteration: ʼEldâʻâh; Pronunciation: el-daw-aw'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God of knowledge;

H421.notes: Transliteration: ʼâlâh; Pronunciation: aw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root (rather identical with H422 (אָלָה) through the idea of invocation);

H422.meaning: properly, to adjure, i.e. (usually in a bad sense) imprecate
H422.notes: Transliteration: ʼâlâh; Pronunciation: aw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H423.notes: Transliteration: ʼâlâh; Pronunciation: aw-law'; Derivation: from H422 (אָלָה);

H424.meaning: an oak or other strong tree
H424.notes: Transliteration: ʼêlâh; Pronunciation: ay-law'; Derivation: feminine of H352 (אַיִל);

H425.meaning: Elah, the name of an Edomite, of four Israelites, and also of a place in Palestine
H425.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊlâh; Pronunciation: ay-law'; Derivation: the same as H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה;

H426.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕlâhh; Pronunciation: el-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H433 (אֱלוֹהַּ);

H427.meaning: an oak or other strong tree
H427.notes: Transliteration: ʼallâh; Pronunciation: al-law'; Derivation: A variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה

H428.kjv: an-(the) other; one sort, so, some, such, them, these (same), they, this, those, thus, which, who(-m).
H428.notes: Transliteration: ʼêl-leh; Pronunciation: ale'-leh; Derivation: prolonged from H411 (אֵל);

H429.notes: Transliteration: ʼêlleh; Pronunciation: ale'-leh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H428 (אֵלֶּה)

H430.meaning: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
H430.kjv: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.
H430.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕlôhîym; Pronunciation: el-o-heem'; Derivation: plural of H433 (אֱלוֹהַּ);

H431.notes: Transliteration: ʼălûw; Pronunciation: al-oo'; Derivation: (Aramaic) probably prolonged from H412 (אֵל);

H432.notes: Transliteration: ʼillûw; Pronunciation: il-loo'; Derivation: probably from H408 (אַל);

H433.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕlôwahh; Pronunciation: el-o'-ah; Derivation: rarely (shortened) אֱלֹהַּ; probably prolonged (emphatic) from H410 (אֵל);

H434.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕlûwl; Pronunciation: el-ool'; Derivation: for H457 (אֱלִיל);

H435.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlûwl; Pronunciation: el-ool'; Derivation: probably of foreign derivation;

H436.meaning: an oak or other strong tree
H436.notes: Transliteration: ʼêlôwn; Pronunciation: ay-lone'; Derivation: prolonged from H352 (אַיִל);

H437.meaning: an oak or other strong tree
H437.notes: Transliteration: ʼallôwn; Pronunciation: al-lone'; Derivation: a variation of H436 (אֵלוֹן)

H438.meaning: Allon, an Israelite, also a place in Palestine
H438.notes: Transliteration: ʼAllôwn; Pronunciation: al-lone'; Derivation: the same as H437 (אַלּוֹן);

H439.notes: Transliteration: ʼAllôwn Bâkûwth; Pronunciation: al-lone' baw-kooth'; Derivation: from H437 (אַלּוֹן) and a variation of H1068 (בְּכִית); oak of weeping;

H440.meaning: an Elonite or descendant (collectively) of Elon
H440.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊlôwnîy; Pronunciation: ay-lo-nee'; Derivation: or rather (shortened) אֵלֹנִי; patron from H438 (אַלּוֹן);

H441.meaning: familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle)
H441.kjv: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox.
H441.notes: Transliteration: ʼallûwph; Pronunciation: al-loof'; Derivation: or (shortened) אַלֻּף; from H502 (אָלַף);

H442.meaning: Alush, a place in the Desert
H442.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂlûwsh; Pronunciation: aw-loosh'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H443.meaning: Elzabad, the name of two Israelites
H443.notes: Transliteration: ʼElzâbâd; Pronunciation: el-zaw-bawd'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2064 (זָבַד); God has bestowed;

H444.meaning: to muddle, i.e. (figuratively and intransitive) to turn (morally) corrupt
H444.notes: Transliteration: ʼâlach; Pronunciation: aw-lakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H445.notes: Transliteration: ʼElchânân; Pronunciation: el-khaw-nawn'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2603 (חָנַן); God (is) gracious;

H446.meaning: Eliab, the name of six Israelites
H446.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʼâb; Pronunciation: el-ee-awb'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1 (אָב); God of (his) father;

H447.meaning: Eliel, the name of nine Israelites
H447.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʼêl; Pronunciation: el-ee-ale'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) repeated; God of (his) God;

H448.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʼâthâh; Pronunciation: el-ee-aw-thaw'; Derivation: or (contraction) אֱלִיָּתָה; from H410 (אֵל) and H225 (אוּת); God of (his) consent;

H449.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîydâd; Pronunciation: el-ee-dawd'; Derivation: from the same as H419 (אֶלְדָּד); God of (his) love;

H450.meaning: Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader
H450.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyâdâʻ; Pronunciation: el-yaw-daw'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God (is) knowing;

H451.meaning: the stout part, i.e. the fat tail of the Oriental sheep
H451.notes: Transliteration: ʼalyâh; Pronunciation: al-yaw'; Derivation: from H422 (אָלָה) (in the original sense of strength);

H452.meaning: Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites
H452.notes: Transliteration: ʼÊlîyâh; Pronunciation: ay-lee-yaw'; Derivation: or prolonged אֵלִיָּהוּ; from H410 (אֵל) and H3050 (יָהּ); God of Jehovah;

H453.meaning: Elihu, the name of one of Job's friends, and of three Israelites
H453.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyhûw; Pronunciation: el-ee-hoo'; Derivation: or (fully) אֱלִיהוּא; from H410 (אֵל) and H1931 (הוּא); God of him;

H454.meaning: Eljehoenai or Eljoenai, the name of seven Israelites
H454.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyᵉhôwʻêynay; Pronunciation: el-ye-ho-ay-nah'ee; Derivation: or (shortened) אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי; from H413 (אֵל) and H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5869 (עַיִן); towards Jehovah (are) my eyes;

H455.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyachbâʼ; Pronunciation: el-yakh-baw'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2244 (חָבָא); God will hide;

H456.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîychôreph; Pronunciation: el-ee-kho'-ref; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2779 (חֹרֶף); God of autumn;

H457.meaning: good for nothing, by anal. vain or vanity; specifically an idol
H457.kjv: idol, no value, thing of nought.
H457.notes: Transliteration: ʼĕlîyl; Pronunciation: el-eel'; Derivation: apparently from H408 (אַל);

H458.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîymelek; Pronunciation: el-ee-meh'-lek; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); God of (the) king;

H459.notes: Transliteration: ʼillêyn; Pronunciation: il-lane'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or shorter אִלֵּן; prolonged from H412 (אֵל);

H460.meaning: Eljasaph, the name of two Israelites
H460.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyâçâph; Pronunciation: el-yaw-sawf'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3254 (יָסַף); God (is) gatherer;

H461.meaning: Eliezer, the name of a Damascene and of ten Israelites
H461.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʻezer; Pronunciation: el-ee-eh'-zer; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); God of help;

H462.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʻêynay; Pronunciation: el-ee-ay-nah'ee; Derivation: probably contracted for H454 (אֶלְיְהוֹעֵינַי);

H463.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyʻâm; Pronunciation: el-ee-awm'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5971 (עַם); God of (the) people;

H464.meaning: Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau
H464.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyphaz; Pronunciation: el-ee-faz'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6337 (פָּז); God of gold;

H465.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyphâl; Pronunciation: el-ee-fawl'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6419 (פָּלַל); God of judgment;

H466.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyphᵉlêhûw; Pronunciation: el-ee-fe-lay'-hoo; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6395 (פָּלָה); God of his distinction;

H467.meaning: Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites
H467.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîypheleṭ; Pronunciation: el-ee-feh'-let; Derivation: or (shortened) אֱלְפֶּלֶט; from H410 (אֵל) and H6405 (פַּלֵּט); God of deliverance;

H468.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîytsûwr; Pronunciation: el-ee-tsoor'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6697 (צוּר); God of (the) rock;

H469.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîytsâphân; Pronunciation: el-ee-tsaw-fawn'; Derivation: or (shortened) אֶלְצָפָן; from H410 (אֵל) and H6845 (צָפַן); God of treasure;

H470.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyqâʼ; Pronunciation: el-ee-kaw'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6958 (קוֹא); God of rejection;

H471.meaning: Eljakim, the name of four Israelites
H471.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyâqîym; Pronunciation: el-yaw-keem'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6965 (קוּם); God of raising;

H472.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyshebaʻ; Pronunciation: el-ee-sheh'-bah; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); God of (the) oath;

H473.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyshâh; Pronunciation: el-ee-shaw'; Derivation: probably of foreign derivation;

H474.meaning: Elishua, the son of King David
H474.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyshûwaʻ; Pronunciation: el-ee-shoo'-ah; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7769 (שׁוּעַ); God of supplication (or of riches);

H475.meaning: Eljashib, the name of six Israelites
H475.notes: Transliteration: ʼElyâshîyb; Pronunciation: el-yaw-sheeb'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7725 (שׁוּב); God will restore;

H476.meaning: Elishama, the name of seven Israelites
H476.notes: Transliteration: ʼĔlîyshâmâʻ; Pronunciation: el-ee-shaw-maw'; Derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H8085 (שָׁמַע); God of hearing;
